feature_name: Harden db.json write concurrency + mobile logo top-left
description: ONE branch, TWO independent items kept as SEPARATE commits/tasks so the risky backend change and the cosmetic frontend change revert independently. ITEM 1 (high-risk, drives review rigor): structurally eliminate the db.json read-modify-write race CLASS that v1.8.0 patched finding-by-finding. Serialize all db.json persistence behind one mechanism (in-process async write-queue/mutex OR single in-memory source-of-truth with atomic serialized persistence — PE chooses and justifies, weighing write-queue/mutex vs. single in-memory SoT with debounced/atomic writes vs. whole-file-with-a-lock + fresh-read-inside-the-lock). Must: make concurrent RMW from ALL writer paths (runScanDirectories across ffmpeg awaits, POST /api/settings, POST /api/progress, recordServed, setTranscodeStatus, POST /api/config background scan) provably non-clobbering, proven by a regression test that interleaves settings + progress + recordServed + a scan and asserts nothing is lost (the v1.8.0 scan-clobber test at test/integration/scan-clobber.test.js is the template); preserve ALL v1.8.0 behavior + keep the 194-test suite green (incl. frozen suites like transcode-cache.test.js); ideally SIMPLIFY the runScanDirectories re-read-merge once a real lock exists but WITHOUT regressing the mount-loss guard / lastServedAt / transcodeStatus fixes (it is load-bearing); make on-disk saves atomic write-temp-then-rename — saveDatabase (server.js:73) is currently a plain fs.writeFileSync (crash mid-write can corrupt db.json). FOLD IN (same code area, both in tech-debt-tracker.md): (a) Active #3 — the FR3.4 dropped-rescan tail: when the coalesced rescan drain budget is exhausted with rescanRequested still set, schedule ONE deferred/rate-limited rescan via a short unref'd timer instead of dropping it; (b) Active #2 (OPTIONAL, only if PE finds it low-risk) — share ONE TRANSCODE_DIR enumeration between sweepAgedTranscodes + evictTranscodeCache instead of two back-to-back readdir+statSync walks. ITEM 2 (cosmetic, low-risk, SEPARATE commit): on MOBILE, move the logo TOP-LEFT on BOTH home and watch/video pages matching desktop; REMOVE the centered .mobile-logo app-shell logo (style.css mobile @media block ~1583-1631 sets header{flex-direction:column} + .header-left{width:100%;justify-content:center} + shows centered .mobile-logo). Desktop is already top-left — leave it. PM/PE decide what happens to mobile search once the logo is no longer centered above it (e.g. logo top-left with search beside/below it) — keep it clean and usable. Bottom-nav app-shell (Home/Playlists/Dark/Settings) STAYS. Preserve a11y (aria-labels, FileTube-home link), safe-area-inset-top, no theme/icon/appearance regression. [MANUAL]-visual (Dean confirms on-device). Repo: Node 22, node:test (npm test), ESLint 0 errors (11 'defined but never used' exported-globals warnings = allowed baseline), additive/zero-regression, ships to deantammam/filetube on a v*.*.* tag. Item 1 gets the FULL pipeline (discovery -> design -> implementation -> build) AND the two-reviewer QA gate (quality-assurance agent + separate /code-review) with v1.8.0 rigor.
npm_env_note: Every node/npm command in this repo needs `export PATH="/home/coder/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin:$PATH"` first (fnm not auto-sourced). Applies to specialist agents running npm test / npm run lint.
prior_release: v1.8.0 settings-automation-cache shipped (stage done in prior state; see docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-05-settings-automation-cache.md). The 194-test suite is the green baseline. This feature makes the v1.8.0 race remediation (FR1 re-read-merge-on-save, FR3.1 unreadable-subtree, FR3.2 persistedServedAt, FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed, FR3.4 bounded rescan drain) STRUCTURAL rather than finding-by-finding.

tasks.title: Item 1 core: serialized updateDatabase(mutatorFn) + atomic saveDatabase + convert all 7 writers + collapse the scan re-read-merge
tasks.description: The headline hardening (two-reviewer QA gate). Add two building blocks to server.js and route all writes through them; readers UNCHANGED. (A) Atomic saveDatabase (server.js:73): rewrite to write a unique SAME-DIR temp file (`${DB_FILE}.${process.pid}.${seq++}.tmp`), fsync the fd, then renameSync over DB_FILE; on any failure clean up the temp (no orphan) and leave the original DB_FILE intact; signature unchanged (saveDatabase(db)); stays SYNCHRONOUS (the mutate-then-save must complete in one tick). (B) Serialized updateDatabase(mutatorFn): a module-level promise chain (let dbWriteChain=Promise.resolve()) where each call does `dbWriteChain.then(() => { const db=loadDatabase(); const result=mutatorFn(db); if(result!==false) saveDatabase(db); return result; })` and reassigns `dbWriteChain = run.catch(()=>{})` so a failed/throwing mutation is ISOLATED and never wedges the chain (caller's promise still rejects, next write proceeds). Contract: mutator is SYNCHRONOUS (no awaits), NON-REENTRANT (must not call updateDatabase), returns false to SKIP the save. Export updateDatabase for tests; place near saveDatabase. Convert all 7 writers per the design's exact recipes: (1) setTranscodeStatus (~415) — mutator sets status only when changed, else return false; (2) recordServed (~448) — KEEP the persistedServedAt ~10min throttle check synchronously BEFORE updateDatabase so throttled Range requests cost ZERO disk reads and never touch the lock; only the due branch enqueues a mutator that re-checks on-disk lastServedAt, sets it, updates the map (optional optimistic map set to coalesce a burst); (3) runScanDirectories final save — see the collapse below; (4) POST /api/config (~1038) — mutator sets db.folders/db.folderSettings from locally-computed validFolders/cleanSettings, respond with the locals (not db), then kick scanDirectories(); (5) POST /api/settings (~1116) — validation stays outside; capture prevInterval + merged `saved` settings via closure inside the mutator, then armScanTimer() only if scanIntervalMinutes changed, respond with settingsResponse(saved); (6) POST /api/progress (~1274) — 400 body validation stays outside, MOVE the 404 existence check INSIDE the mutator against the fresh db (set a notFound flag + return false, then 404 after await); (7) DELETE /api/videos/:id (~1296) — KEEP today's ordering for the 500-on-FS-failure contract: initial loadDatabase read for 404+filePath, FS unlinks inside the existing try/catch (throw => 500, db untouched), THEN updateDatabase deletes db.metadata[id]/db.progress[id]. recordServed + setTranscodeStatus stay fire-and-forget. COLLAPSE the scan Phase-2 re-read-merge: scan keeps its TWO-PHASE shape (NEVER hold the lock across FFmpeg awaits) — Phase 1 (no lock, unchanged): loadDatabase snapshot, awaited extraction building newMetadata, missingRoots/unreadablePaths, selectPrunableIds, idempotent FS cleanup of pruned thumbnails/transcodes; Phase 2 (ONE updateDatabase mutator): REMOVE the explicit `const fresh = loadDatabase()` (~872) — the mutator's db IS the fresh-inside-the-lock read; run the reconcile loop (root backfill + FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed from db.metadata[id] + reconcileTranscode) + mergeScannedMetadata(db.metadata,newMetadata) + db.progress prune + clearPersistedServedAt(id) inside it, return dbChanged. This provably CLOSES the hair-thin fresh-read->save window WITHOUT regressing: mount-loss guard (selectPrunableIds still off the scan's missingRoots), lastServedAt on-disk authority (mergeScannedMetadata only advances, never regresses), FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed (from the locked fresh db), bounded rescan drain (MAX_RESCAN_FOLLOWUPS unchanged — the tail itself is T2). reconcileTranscode is audited safe inside the mutator (only fs.existsSync reads + in-place mutation; no db writes, no queue kicks, non-reentrant). Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md's 'Re-read-merge-on-save' key-decision entry to the new contract: (i) all writers serialize through updateDatabase, (ii) saveDatabase is atomic write-temp-then-rename, (iii) the scan still merges into fresh state under the lock rather than overwriting a start-of-scan snapshot. Do NOT edit the completed v1.8.0 exec plan. Fold-in #2 (double readdir) is DEFERRED (not this task). The deferred-rescan tail (#3) is T2.
tasks.done_when: updateDatabase + atomic saveDatabase implemented per the design contract (serialized, non-reentrant, mutator-returns-false-skips-save, failed mutation isolated so the chain never wedges; temp-fsync-rename with on-fail cleanup + original intact). All 7 writers converted per the exact recipes (recordServed throttle BEFORE updateDatabase = zero disk read on throttled Range; /api/progress 404 inside mutator; DELETE keeps FS-unlink-before-db 500 ordering; /api/settings prevInterval/saved via closure + armScanTimer only on interval change). Scan re-read-merge collapsed into ONE Phase-2 mutator with the explicit fresh=loadDatabase removed and NO lock held across FFmpeg awaits. HEADLINE [INTEGRATION] test: settings + progress + recordServed + a scan interleaved in one tick, all awaited, ALL survive (settings change, progress entry, lastServedAt, and the scan's new-file metadata) — FAILS against pre-serialization code, PASSES after. [UNIT] atomic-write: forced write/rename failure leaves prior db.json intact + no *.tmp orphan; a successful save yields complete JSON + no leftover *.tmp. [UNIT] serialization-correctness: two back-to-back updateDatabase calls mutating DIFFERENT fields both survive, mutators run in enqueue order each seeing the prior's committed state. [INTEGRATION] v1.8.0 invariants re-verified: scan-clobber/scan-api/scan-prune/age-sweep green unmodified in behavior + targeted asserts that mount-loss guard, FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed, and lastServedAt authority still hold. transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN/byte-identical; all 194 existing tests green; every timer unref'd (no hung process; no lock held across an await so parallel node:test can't deadlock); ARCHITECTURE.md key-decision updated; lint 0 errors (no new warnings beyond the 11 baseline). npm env PATH exported before npm.
tasks.result: VERIFIED READY (build-specialist, deep scrutiny). lint 0; npm test 201/201; clean exit (no hang — mutex chain always settles, no lock held across an await). Confirmed: updateDatabase serialized primitive (fresh-read-INSIDE-lock, failure-isolated, chain never wedges); atomic saveDatabase (temp->fsync->rename; BOTH writeFileSync-fail and renameSync-fail paths leave db.json byte-identical + no orphan temp); all 7 writers converted correctly (recordServed throttle BEFORE lock = zero disk read on throttled Range; DELETE keeps FS-unlink-before-db 500 contract; POST /api/settings closure prevInterval + re-arm only on interval change; /api/progress 404 inside mutator); scan Phase-1 FFmpeg work OUTSIDE the lock, Phase-2 collapsed into ONE synchronous mutator with NO regression to mount-loss / lastServedAt / FR3.3 transcodeStatus / bounded drain; headline 4-way interleave test is a REAL guard (verified it fails against the bare-mutex stale-read flaw); transcode-cache.test.js byte-identical + readers unchanged. Diff scoped to server.js + docs/ARCHITECTURE.md + 4 test files.

tasks.title: Item 1 fold-in #3: deferred-rescan tail
tasks.description: Tech-debt Active #3. Add scheduleDeferredRescan(): a module-level single-guarded (`if (deferredRescanTimer) return;`), unref'd, ~5s (DEFERRED_RESCAN_DELAY_MS=5000) rate-limited setTimeout that clears its own handle then re-enters the already-bounded scanDirectories().catch(console.error). Hook it into scanDirectories's finally: read rescanRequested BEFORE clearing scanning, and call scheduleDeferredRescan() when it is still set (drain budget spent). Add a currentDeferredRescanTimer() test accessor. Bounded by construction: at most one pending timer, unref'd (never keeps the process/test alive), 5s rate-limited, re-enters the bounded scan — self-heals in discrete 5s passes under sustained demand, fires once and stops under bounded demand. Resolves the documented 'auto-scan Off + folder-add during the single follow-up pass' drop. Part of the HARDENING commit (may fold into T1's commit — EM/coordinator's call at commit time), NOT the logo commit. Fold-in #2 stays DEFERRED (tracker #2 open; its conditional AC is N/A).
tasks.done_when: [UNIT/INTEGRATION] with scanIntervalMinutes Off, drive scanDirectories so the drain exits with rescanRequested still true; exactly ONE unref'd timer scheduled (currentDeferredRescanTimer() non-null; a second exhaustion does NOT stack a second timer); running the deferred pass indexes the pending folder; timer cleared in teardown (suite exits clean, no hung process). The bounded rescan drain (MAX_RESCAN_FOLLOWUPS) is not regressed. transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN; full suite green (201 + new); lint 0. Also update tech-debt-tracker.md: move Active #3 to Closed with this resolution; leave Active #2 OPEN (deferred).

tasks.title: Item 2 (SEPARATE commit): mobile logo top-left
tasks.description: CSS-only, low-risk, its OWN commit (not the hardening commit); no two-reviewer QA gate, build-specialist verify + Dean [MANUAL] on-device only. In public/css/style.css's mobile @media (max-width:768px) 'Mobile app shell: header restructure' block (~1583-1631), two edits: (1) .header-left: justify-content center -> flex-start (keep width:100%, gap:0); (2) .mobile-logo img: margin 0 auto -> 0 (keep display:block, height/width:28px). Header stays flex-direction:column so .header-search stays width:100% full-width on the row BELOW the logo. Update the block's lead comment from 'Centered logo...' to 'Top-left logo + full-width search below it'. .logo stays hidden, .mobile-logo stays shown, .header-right stays hidden, bottom-nav untouched. Covers home (index.html) + watch (watch.html) via the shared header/stylesheet; also check setup.html (same header, no .header-search) for visual consistency. Preserve a11y (aria-label='FileTube home', tab order — DOM untouched), safe-area-inset-top (header padding/min-height untouched), no theme/icon-set regression. Desktop (>768px) provably unchanged (edit inside the mobile media query). No public/js/*.js selector breaks (no class/id/DOM change).
tasks.done_when: [MANUAL] logo renders top-left on home + watch (matching desktop), search usable full-width below, aria-label/link-to-home + tab order unchanged, safe-area-inset-top respected, no icon-set/theme regression, desktop unchanged — Dean confirms on-device. [PROCESS] lint 0 (0 new warnings) + npm test green (no existing test asserting header markup breaks). Change scoped to the mobile media query only.

grounding.saveDatabase: server.js:73 — plain fs.writeFileSync(DB_FILE, JSON.stringify(db,null,2)); NOT atomic. 7 saveDatabase() call sites confirmed: lines 419, 460, 899, 1041, 1144, 1291, 1329.
grounding.scan_clobber_template: test/integration/scan-clobber.test.js (v1.8.0 FR1 headline). Also merge/lifecycle tests in test/integration/scan-api.test.js, test/integration/scan-prune.test.js, test/integration/age-sweep.test.js.
grounding.mobile_logo_css: public/css/style.css:1583-1631 (mobile @media block: header flex-direction:column, .header-left width:100% justify-content:center, .logo display:none, .mobile-logo display:block centered via margin:0 auto, .header-right display:none). Desktop header (top-left) unchanged.
grounding.tech_debt_fold_in: docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md Active #3 (FR3.4 dropped-rescan tail -> deferred unref'd rescan) FOLD IN; Active #2 (double readdir+statSync over TRANSCODE_DIR) OPTIONAL fold-in per PE risk call.

history.note: New feature initiated after v1.8.0 (settings-automation-cache) shipped/done. Prior state archived by overwrite; v1.8.0 exec plan is in docs/exec-plans/completed/. Read CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, RELIABILITY.md, tech-debt-tracker.md, the mobile CSS block, and grounded the two items in real code before routing: (1) saveDatabase (server.js:73) is a plain non-atomic writeFileSync with 7 call sites — the crash-safety atomic-write ask is a real gap; the v1.8.0 re-read-merge (FR1) still has a hair-thin lost-update window and is finding-by-finding, so a single serialized persistence path is the structural fix. (2) Mobile centered-logo confirmed at style.css:1583-1631. Tech-debt Active #2 (double readdir) and #3 (dropped-rescan tail) confirmed in the tracker for fold-in. ONE branch harden/db-writes-and-logo, TWO independent commits (Item 1 high-risk hardening drives the two-reviewer gate; Item 2 cosmetic mobile logo). Target v1.9.0.

Coordinator explicitly authorized starting Discovery and asked the EM to return with requirements/acceptance criteria + open questions (esp. the PE's proposed hardening approach). Advanced bootstrap -> discovery. Wrote product-manager inbox at .state/inbox/product-manager.md grounding the PM in: the two-item split with SEPARATE-commit constraint; the full writer-path list + the non-clobbering interleave regression test requirement; the atomic-write gap at saveDatabase:73; the fold-in of tech-debt #2/#3; the v1.8.0 behavior/tests that MUST be preserved; and the mobile logo decision (already made by Dean — do NOT re-ask; decide only the search layout). Asked the PM to keep D1-mobile-logo settled, tag every AC [UNIT]/[INTEGRATION]/[MANUAL]/[PROCESS], keep the two items' AC in separate sections, and surface the PE hardening-approach choice as the headline open question for the design gate (write-queue/mutex vs. in-memory SoT vs. locked whole-file). Exec plan to be written to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-05-harden-db-writes-and-logo.md; artifacts stay null until the PM produces it. Awaiting PM completion, then /prep-pe-design.

Design complete: PE wrote the ## Design section into docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-05-harden-db-writes-and-logo.md and set artifacts.design to that path. HEADLINE RECOMMENDATION (for the design-gate sign-off): a serialized in-process updateDatabase(mutatorFn) primitive — an async-mutex (promise chain) that per write loads a FRESH db inside the lock, applies the synchronous mutator, and atomically saves; all 7 writers route through it. This is approach (c) [locked whole-file + fresh-read-inside-lock] packaged as approach (a)'s clean primitive; it provably kills the RMW clobber class (read+write in one critical section) at the LOWEST blast radius. Rejected (b) single in-memory SoT (rewrites all ~15 loadDatabase() reader contracts + crash-divergence risk) and bare (a) (leaves stale-read window unless the read moves inside the lock — which IS the chosen primitive). Decisive win vs (b): readers are UNCHANGED (loadDatabase stays; atomic rename means no torn reads). ALL saves become atomic write-temp-then-rename (fsync + same-dir temp + rename + on-fail cleanup). The v1.8.0 re-read-merge SIMPLIFIES (subsumed): scan keeps its two-phase shape (never holds the lock across FFmpeg awaits); the explicit fresh=loadDatabase() is removed and the reconcile+mergeScannedMetadata+progress-prune+save collapse into ONE updateDatabase mutator, provably closing the hair-thin fresh-read->save window — mount-loss guard, lastServedAt authority, FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed, and bounded rescan drain all preserved with tests kept. recordServed's persistedServedAt throttle stays a throttle-only check BEFORE updateDatabase (no per-Range disk read/lock). Fold-in #3 (MANDATORY) designed: deferred-rescan tail via a single-guarded, unref'd, 5s rate-limited setTimeout re-entering the bounded scanDirectories. Fold-in #2 (double readdir) DEFERRED — would touch the frozen transcode-cache path; tracker #2 stays open, conditional AC N/A. Tests specified: headline 4-way interleave (fail-pre/pass-post), atomic-write unit, serialization-correctness, v1.8.0-invariant re-verify, deferred-rescan-tail. Item 2 (mobile logo): two-line CSS change in the mobile @media block (.header-left justify-content center->flex-start; .mobile-logo img margin 0 auto->0), search stays full-width below, desktop/a11y/safe-area/bottom-nav untouched. Recommended task split: Task 1 core serialize+atomic+7-writer conversion+scan simplify (two-reviewer QA gate); Task 2 fold-in #3 deferred tail; Task 3 Item 2 logo — Item 1 and Item 2 as SEPARATE commits. Noted ARCHITECTURE.md's re-read-merge-on-save key-decision must be updated WITH Task 1 to describe the new contract. No new runtime dependency (hand-rolled promise chain suffices). Next: EM relays the recommended approach to Dean for the design-gate sign-off, then /prep-em-tasks.

Discovery complete: PM wrote docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-05-harden-db-writes-and-logo.md (both items separate sections; Item 1 = 8 tagged AC incl. the headline non-clobbering interleave test modeled on scan-clobber.test.js, atomic write-temp-then-rename behavior, explicit v1.8.0-invariant re-verification, mandatory deferred-rescan-tail fold-in #3, and conditional shared-enumeration #2; Item 2 = 7 [MANUAL] + 1 [PROCESS] AC, search-full-width-below-logo proposed default). artifacts.requirements/exec_plan already set by PM to that path. No new product decision surfaced; logo decision stays locked; search sub-question defaulted. Advanced discovery -> design per coordinator. Wrote principal-engineer inbox at .state/inbox/principal-engineer.md. HEADLINE design deliverable = the PE's RECOMMENDED serialization approach + justification, to be relayed to Dean at the design gate BEFORE build (it reshapes how the whole app reads/writes db.json). Framed the three approaches with the coordinator's sharpened tradeoffs: (a) write-queue/mutex — but the READ must also move inside the lock at every site or the stale-read window remains (lower-blast-radius is partly illusory); (b) single in-memory SoT — most fully eliminates the race but changes every loadDatabase() reader's contract; (c) locked whole-file + fresh-read-inside-the-lock — closest evolution of the v1.8.0 re-read-merge but keeps full-file I/O per write and little complexity reduction. ALL three must also deliver atomic write-temp-then-rename for saveDatabase. Asked the PE to specify: chosen approach + why; how each of the 7 writer sites + all readers change; how the v1.8.0 re-read-merge simplifies-or-stays WITHOUT regressing mount-loss/lastServedAt/transcodeStatus; the deferred-rescan-tail (#3) impl; the double-readdir (#2) fold-or-defer call; the atomic-write impl; coherence with recordServed's in-memory persistedServedAt throttle; and the regression tests. Plus an Item 2 design (logo top-left, search full-width below) and a task breakdown keeping hardening and logo as SEPARATE tasks/commits. Emphasized: high-risk persistence-layer code — conservative design, all 194 tests green, frozen suites untouched. artifacts.design stays null until the PE writes the ## Design section. Coordinator will run the principal-engineer and relay the recommended approach to Dean for a design-gate sign-off before implementation; then /prep-em-tasks.

DESIGN GATE PASSED: Dean signed off — GO with the PE's serialized updateDatabase(mutatorFn) approach (locked load-fresh-inside-lock -> synchronous mutate -> atomic write-temp-then-rename; all 7 writers routed through it; readers unchanged; scan re-read-merge collapses into the mutator with FFmpeg work OUTSIDE the lock; fold in #3 deferred-rescan tail; #2 double-readdir deferred). Approved as-is, no changes. EM-owned task-breakdown stage done: adopted the PE's recommended split, recorded T1/T2/T3 in state.tasks (each with description/files/done_when/status) and wrote the ## Task breakdown section into the exec plan. Item 1 (hardening) and Item 2 (logo) stay SEPARATE commits: T1 = the core serialize+atomic+7-writer-conversion+scan-collapse (two-reviewer QA gate), T2 = fold-in #3 deferred-rescan tail (same hardening commit as T1 or a follow-on — coordinator's call at commit time, NOT the logo commit), T3 = Item 2 mobile logo (its own commit, build-verify + Dean [MANUAL] only). Advanced design -> implementation; set T1 status=in-progress (T2/T3 pending). Wrote software-developer inbox at .state/inbox/software-developer.md for T1 ONLY, grounded in the design's exact updateDatabase/saveDatabase snippets + the 7 per-site conversion recipes + the two-phase scan collapse (remove explicit fresh=loadDatabase at ~872; lock wraps only the synchronous merge+save, never the FFmpeg awaits) + the required ARCHITECTURE.md key-decision update + all 4 test groups (headline 4-way interleave fail-pre/pass-post, atomic-write unit, serialization-correctness, v1.8.0-invariant re-verify). Hard constraints reiterated: mutator synchronous + non-reentrant + returns-false-skips-save + failed-mutation-isolated; recordServed persistedServedAt throttle check BEFORE updateDatabase (zero disk read on throttled Range); /api/progress 404 inside the mutator; DELETE keeps FS-unlink-before-db 500 ordering; /api/settings prevInterval/saved via closure + armScanTimer only on interval change; no lock held across an await (parallel node:test can't deadlock); all timers unref'd; transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN; all 194 tests green; lint 0; PATH export before npm. Scoped OUT of T1: the deferred-rescan tail (=T2), the logo CSS (=T3), fold-in #2 (deferred). Coordinator will run the software-developer against T1, then drive the per-task implement->build loop + the two-reviewer gate on the hardening. Awaiting T1 completion, then /prep-build-verify.

T1 (core hardening) DONE + build-verified with DEEP SCRUTINY (coordinator-relayed): build-specialist verdict VERIFIED READY. lint 0; npm test 201/201; clean exit (no hang — mutex chain always settles, no lock held across an await). Confirmed: updateDatabase serialized primitive (fresh-read-INSIDE-lock, failure-isolated, chain never wedges); atomic saveDatabase (temp->fsync->rename; BOTH writeFileSync-fail and renameSync-fail paths leave db.json byte-identical + no orphan temp); all 7 writers converted correctly (recordServed throttle BEFORE lock = zero disk read on throttled Range; DELETE keeps FS-unlink-before-db 500 contract; POST /api/settings closure prevInterval + re-arm only on interval change; /api/progress 404 inside mutator); scan Phase-1 FFmpeg work OUTSIDE the lock, Phase-2 collapsed into ONE synchronous mutator with NO regression to mount-loss / lastServedAt / FR3.3 transcodeStatus / bounded drain; headline 4-way interleave test is a REAL guard (fails against the bare-mutex stale-read flaw); transcode-cache.test.js byte-identical + readers unchanged. Diff scoped to server.js + docs/ARCHITECTURE.md + 4 test files. Marked T1 status=done with result recorded. Advanced to T2 (deferred-rescan tail, tech-debt #3, part of the HARDENING commit — NOT the logo): set T2 status=in-progress. Re-read the CURRENT drain seam (line-shifted by T1): scanState (server.js:777), MAX_RESCAN_FOLLOWUPS (789), scanDirectories (804) with the do/while drain (812-816) and the finally clearing scanning (817-820) — the bounded drain exits with scanState.rescanRequested possibly STILL true (budget spent), which is exactly the FR3.4 drop the tail fixes. Wrote software-developer inbox for T2 ONLY: add scheduleDeferredRescan() — module-level single-guarded (`if (deferredRescanTimer) return;`), unref'd, DEFERRED_RESCAN_DELAY_MS=5000 setTimeout that nulls its own handle then re-enters scanDirectories().catch(console.error); hook it into scanDirectories's finally by reading scanState.rescanRequested BEFORE clearing scanning and calling scheduleDeferredRescan() when still set; add currentDeferredRescanTimer() accessor. Bounded/no-livelock (drain still bounded per invocation; at most one pending timer; 5s rate-limited; re-enters the bounded scan) and no-dangling-timer/clean-exit under node:test (unref + teardown clear). Regression test: drain budget exhausted + rescan still pending => exactly one deferred follow-up fires and indexes the pending work, no dangling timer/clean exit, no second stacked timer on a second exhaustion. Also move tech-debt Active #3 -> Closed; leave Active #2 (double readdir) OPEN/DEFERRED per the design. Constraints reiterated: full suite green (201 + new), transcode-cache.test.js frozen, PATH export before npm, do NOT touch T3 (logo). Awaiting T2 completion, then /prep-build-verify; after T2 build-verifies, the two-reviewer QA gate runs on the COMBINED T1+T2 hardening, then T3 (logo).
